info.title: KiSoft One API
info.description:
Last Modified: 2024-06-26 14:00:00 This document represents the API description for the KiSoft One product. # Introduction This API is documented in **OpenAPI Specification** (OAS) format. In addition to standard OpenAPI syntax a few [vendor extensions](https://github.com/Redocly/redoc/blob/main/docs/redoc-vendor-extensions.md) are used for easier documentation. ## Encoding The standard UTF-8 is used for the character encoding for the data interchange between KiSoft One and the higher-level system. ## General Conventions Throughout this documentation, `monospace letters` are used for data definitions (fields, values, etc...). When referencing certain values of data fields, every `"value"` is written in quotation marks. All data fields are named in `camelCase` schema with **lowercase first letter**. All component references in the OAS schema are named in `PascalCase` schema with **uppercase first letter**. > Since this specification can be used on non HTTP transport layers, it omits the use of URI parameters (e.g. `PATCH /frameworkRoute/{clientNumber}/{orderNumber}`). ## Transmission of Date & Time A date attribute has to be transmitted as string in the following format: `YYYY-MM-DD`, e. g. `2024-03-15`. A time attribute has to be transmitted as string in the following format: `HH:MM:SS`, e. g. `14:30:00`. A date-time attribute has to be transmitted as string in the following format: `YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ`, e.g. `2024-01-24T14:15:22Z`. ## Transmission of Identifying Attributes For the transmission of identifying attributes in the openAPI messages the following rules must be followed: - for messages with clientNumber: for the same clientNumber each value of the identifying attribute (e. g. orderNumber, inboundDeliveryNumber, requestNumber) must be unique among all types of messages for this attribute. - for messages without clientNumber: each value of the identifying attribute (e. g. requestNumber of message requestStorageReport) must be globally unique among all types of messages. ## Transport Layer Security It is highly recommended to use HTTPS as the default transport layer for communication. Nevertheless, the interface may be used without any transport layer security. > Note that using the interface without transport layer security is not recommended and should only be used in isolated network environments to ensure communication interference is prevented. ## Application Layer Security This interface allows for application layer security by providing authentication methods. Accessing the interface can be restricted to authenticated communication partners. The following authentication flows are provided. ### Client Credentials Flow With machine-to-machine (M2M) applications, such as this host interface, the system authenticates and authorizes another app rather than a user. For this scenario, typical authentication schemes like username + password or social logins don't make sense. Instead, M2M apps use the Client Credentials Flow ([defined in OAuth 2.0 RFC 6749, section 4.4](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6749#section-4.4)), in which they pass along their Client ID and Client Secret to authenticate themselves and get a token. The flow contains the following interaction steps: 1. Your app authenticates with the Authorization Server using its Client ID and Client Secret (/oauth/token endpoint). 2. Authorization Server validates the Client ID and Client Secret. 3. Authorization Server responds with an Access Token. 4. Your application can use the Access Token to call an API on behalf of itself. 5. The API responds with requested data. ## Using the Interface on Non-HTTP Transport Layers > JSON and/or XML messages on e.g. simple TCP/IP connections without the URL and HTTP-method have to compensate for missing information. > A **corresponding encapsulation element/object** has to be added in order to achieve this. > > Responses also have to compensate for the missing http layer information, which is added in as additional fields (see examples below). **JSON Examples:** WEB-JSON: POST /packUnit/updateSession ```JSON { "clientNumber": "DEFAULT", "sessionAction": "SET" } ``` *TCP-JSON:* ```JSON "PostPackUnitUpdateSession": { "clientNumber": "DEFAULT", "sessionAction": "SET" } ``` Response: ```JSON "PostPackUnitUpdateSession_Response": { "http": 200, "body": {...} } ```
**XML Examples:** WEB-XML: POST /packUnit/updateSession ```XML ``` TCP-XML: ```XML ``` Response: ```XML ``` For a further example see [Example XML Message](#xml). ## Base URL for Communication The base URL for the communication with KiSoft One for incoming messages is: `/kisoft/oneapi/v1/` Incoming URL examples (KiSoft One is server): * `/kisoft/oneapi/v1/customer` with html-method "PUT" * `/kisoft/oneapi/v1/goodsOutOrder` with html-method "POST" * `/kisoft/oneapi/v1/relocationRequest/loadUnits` with html-method "POST" ## Websocket Response Channel KiSoft One acts per default as server - and the higher-level system needs to establish permanent Websocket connections. In order to provide response messages without the need for the higher level system to create a multitude of webhook endpoints a websocket connection is provided. The websockets can be requested at the following paths: * `/kisoft/oneapi/v1/websocket/MovementData` for all events regarding movement data * `/kisoft/oneapi/v1/websocket/StockChanges` for all live stock changes * `/kisoft/oneapi/v1/websocket/Reports` for requested reports and stock snapshots It is only possible to create one socket connection per endpoint. Since the websocket connection is no longer a http transport layer, modifications to the messages described in [Using the Interface on Non-HTTP Transport Layers](#non-http-transport) apply. In order to detect connection loss during idle communication times, the heartbeat message is sent every 60 seconds if no other messages are sent during this period. If the heartbeat or other messages are not acknowledged by the higher level system, the connection is terminated by KiSoft and a new connection has to be established by the higher level system. #### Option: KiSoft One is Client for all Outgoing Reply Messages If it is required, that the higher-level system acts as server and KiSoft One acts as client for all outgoing reply messages, which are marked with "EVENT", then the higher-level system needs to define the base URL warehouse specific. Knapp will add the reply path based on the message in cammelCase. Outgoing URL example: * `https://10.89.17.1/one2host/oneapi/v1/goodsOutOrderReply` # Basic Data Transmission Schema KiSoft processes two different types of data: - movement data - master data ## Movement Data Movement data is data that is only needed for a certain short period of time and changes frequently. Examples of movement data are: data on stock levels, orders, data on processing progress. Movement data is always transmitted in single entities. > Movement data often references master data. Therefore all relevant master data must be transmitted by the higher-level system, before movement data are transmitted. > Otherwise movement data might not be processed correctly. ### Add New Movement Data ### To add new movement data (e. g. an inbound delivery) the POST operation is utilized. ### Updating Already Present Movement Data To update movement data (e.g. a specific goods-out order) with new information, the PATCH operation is utilized. > What information can or cannot be updated, depends on the `processingStatus` of the order/request. Details are stated in the description of the corresponding operation. ### Delete Movement Data ### To add delete movement data (e. g. an a specific goods-out order) the DELETE operation is utilized. For details on how update transmissions are interpreted, see [Communicating Changes to Existing Data](#changes). ## Master Data Master data is data that remains unchanged over a longer period of time and contains information that is needed again and again. It contains information on basic characteristics of units necessary for warehouse operation. Examples of entities for which master data is recorded in KiSoft are: Article, supplier, customer, framework route. This data is often used complementary to movement data and contains data that is required for certain functions or for display. Master data transmissions may contain up to 10000 entities. For big transmissions a delay of several seconds for the response may occur. In normal operation it is assumed that the higher-level system only transmits delta information, adding and deleting master data as changes occur. ### Add new Master Data ### To add new master data (e. g. a specific pack unit) the PUT operation is utilized. ### Updating Already Present Master Data ### To update master data (e.g. a specific pack unit) with new information, the entity has to be retransmitted via the PUT operation. KiSoft will update an existing master data entity or create a new one if it was not already present. For details on how update transmissions are interpreted, see [Communicating Changes to Existing Data](#changes). For details on how to start and close a master data update session, see [processUpdateSession](#operation/processUpdateSession). > **NOTE:** Master data changes are processed by KiSoft regardless of active orders. > > Depending on the degree of progress of an order and depending on the functional relationship between the order and the master data, changes to the master data may or may not have an effect to that order. > > In order to avoid unexpected effects, KNAPP recommends that changes to master data are only made for entities not used in active orders. Some Examples: | Update of data | Possible effects on orders| | --- | --- | | Weight data of a pack unit is updated |
  • weight checks of orders might go wrong and orders have to be manually checked | | for an article `isLotRequired` or `isDateMarkRequired` or `isSerialNumberRequired` is updated to `true` |
  • already active orders might not be able to produce the needed information at all
  • orders with this article might be diverted to checking stations| | route changes dispatch ramps |
  • already active orders might end up on the wrong dispatch ramp | | customer information changed |
  • when KiSoft generates print information, the documents with wrong data might be added to the order| The above examples show only a small fraction of possible effects. It is not possible to determine all possible effects on orders in general because the warehouse layout and logistics play a big role in those effects. ### Delete Master Data ### To delete new master data (e. g. a specific pack unit) the DELETE operation is utilized. ## Communicating Changes to Existing Data When changes have to be communicated either way, the entity that was changed has to be transmitted again. It is not required to retransmit all data for already existing entities. Instead the following behavior applies: * If previously transmitted data has to be **changed** by KiSoft → the field hast to be transmitted with **new data**. * Arrays (like `additionalProperties` except all fields ending with `...Lines`) have to be transmitted with all information still relevant (missing entries are taken off the record). * If previously transmitted data has to be **deleted** by KiSoft → the field hast to be transmitted **empty**. * If previously transmitted data has to be **preserved** by KiSoft → the field has to be **omitted**. Example: If some data has to be deleted from KiSoft (e.g. the `reservationCode` was removed via the user interface from a stock object ) the field `reservationCode` is transmitted empty communicating the change. Similarly, if the higher-level system likes to update an order or request, all fields that are transmitted, are updated accordingly and all fields missing do not change the current record. ### Changing Line Information > **Important!** Information on lines (all fields ending with `...Lines`, e.g. `inboundDeliveryLines`) are handled slightly differently! When updating an order/request via the PATCH operation and changing lines, it is not necessary to transmit the whole array. Since lines are identified via `lineReference` it is only necessary to transmit lines, that have to be added or deleted. If no lines are transmitted, no lines are changed. The following behavior applies: * **Adding new lines:** If a line has to be added, the `addLine` field is used (e.g. `addInboundDeliveryLines`) and the higher-level system has to transmit a line with a new `lineReference` (not used before by this order/request). All transmitted fields are added to the record. * **Deleting existing lines:** If a line has to be 'deleted', the `deleteLine` field is used (`deleteLinesByReference`) with the corresponding `lineReference`. * **Updating existing lines:** To update an existing line, the line has to been deleted (`deleteLinesByReference`) with the corresponding `lineReference` and added again with the same `lineReference` (e.g. `addInboundDeliveryLines`). ## Update Sessions for Master Data Transmission In some circumstances it may be necessary for the higher-level system to do a *fresh* transmission of all master data ignoring any present data and therefore cleaning up old data records. A separate session will be tracked per endpoint (master data type) and `clientNumber` (if transmitted). > In short: the higher level system can open a session with `SET`, transmit all still valid data and close the session afterwards with `CLEANUP`. All records not transmitted between the `SET` and `CLEANUP` messages, will be deleted for the master data type and client. Master data messages are processed independent of an open update session. Changes will be applied as the messages arrive. The big difference comes to play, when the update session is closed. When a session is closed with `CLEANUP`, all master data entities, that were not included transmitted, are deleted from KiSoft. This behavior also implies that in a session delete messages can be omitted. >**NOTE!:** If an update session is opened/closed without client, this message overwrites any client specific sessions. It is recommended to either use update sessions either with or without `clientNumber`. **Mixing messages** with and without `clientNumber` is not recommended and **can lead to data loss**. The following behavior for master data records apply, which are not transmitted since the last `SET`: * customer: Customer master data records are marked as deleted and are not shown at the master data GUI sreens. If references to this customer master data exists (open/active/finished orders), the reference is still valid and will be shown. New order records with that customer will get rejected, since the customer is marked to get deleted. * packUnit: If orders/stock related to the packUnit (product bundle) exist, the record will not get deleted (also not marked for deletion). * packUnitLocationAssignment: If orders or stock exist, the packUnitLocationAssignment is marked to get deleted, additionally replenishment is blocked for this location. For WCS warehouses there is an option, which allows to delete the related stock (only for quantity managed locations like Central Belt System). ### General Master Data Transmission Schema The following sequence diagram showcases the transmission of all types of masterdata. ![General Master Data Transmission](./img/generalMasterDataTransmission.svg) Used Messages: [PutPackUnits](#operation/PutPackUnits), [DeletePackUnits](#operation/DeletePackUnits), [PostPackUnitUpdateSession](#operation/PostPackUnitUpdateSession)

tags.name: Masterdata-Article
tags.description:
Article master data is data that is directly assigned to various articles such as article number, dimensions, weight or optical codes. The main entity for article masterdata is `packUnit`

> **Important:** Article master data can only be created via the host interface by creating a new pack unit with this article, it can also only be deleted by the host interface.
> For existing articles, new pack units may be created directly via GUI also. These user created pack units must then be updated/deleted via user interface.

tags.name: Masterdata-Partner
tags.description:
Partner master data contains information about the (business) partners (customers, suppliers), which are used for the following functions in the KNAPP system:
  - Display
  - Print Data Generation
  - Tour Management
  - Order Release
  - Control Tasks

   > **Important:** Partner master data can only be created or deleted via the host interface.

tags.name: Masterdata-Route
tags.description:
The delivery of orders can be planned with framework routes.
Framework route master data contains information controlling shipping of orders ( route number, dispatch ramps and departure time).
These can also influence the time at which the order starts on KiSoft One.

tags.name: Goods-Out
tags.description: This section provides messages for the goods picking/ outbound processes

tags.name: Goods-In
tags.description: This section provides messages for the inbound processes.

tags.name: Inventory
tags.description: This section provides messages regarding warehouse internal inventory routines.

tags.name: Stock-Changes
tags.description:
This section provides a number of messages dedicated to communicate changes in stock levels.
Whenever stock levels are changed unexpectedly or stock is manipulated outside of normal goods-out processes, a stock change message is sent to the higher-level system.

tags.name: Stock-Reports
tags.description:
This section provides messages to request detailed stock information and followup replies.

> ***NOTE*** clientNumber is optional

tags.name: Capacity-Reports
tags.description: This section provides messages to request detailed storage system capacity information.

tags.name: LoadUnit-Movements
tags.description: This section provides messages to request and notify internal and/or external relocations of load units.

tags.name: External-Processing
tags.description: This section provides messages to allow external (e.g. HOST) systems, to notify KiSoft of a relevant order or load unit processing information.

tags.name: general
tags.description: This section provides general supplementary messages.

tags.name: WS-MovementData
tags.description: This section includes all Messages that are transmitted via the `/websocket/MovementData` endpoint.

tags.name: WS-StockChanges
tags.description: This section includes all Messages that are transmitted via the `/websocket/StockChanges` endpoint.

tags.name: WS-Reports
tags.description: This section includes all Messages that are transmitted via the `/websocket/Reports` endpoint.

tags.name: Inbound Deliveries Usecases
tags.description: This section provides information on inbound processes and describes how different messages are processed and timed. Relevant data is highlighted for each use case. Inbound processes result in increased stock levels. ## Processing Inbound Deliveries Processing inbound deliveries involves receiving and generating stock and transferring the received goods into storage locations. Articles may be repacked into different load units during this process. **Master Data Prerequisites:** For all pack units that have to be received, the following restrictions apply - at least one pack unit of the article that has to be received has to be present at KiSoft See [PostInboundDelivery](#operation/PostInboundDelivery) for details. ![processing inbound deliveries](./img/inboundDelivery.svg) Used Messages: [PostInboundDelivery](#operation/PostInboundDelivery), [PatchInboundDelivery](#operation/PatchInboundDelivery), [PostInboundDeliveryReply](#operation/PostInboundDeliveryReply), [DeleteInboundDelivery](#operation/DeleteInboundDelivery), [PostStockReceived](#operation/PostStockReceived), [PostStorageOrderReply](#operation/PostStorageOrderReply) The message postStorageOrderReply can also be sent before the message postInboundDeliveryReply with the processingStatus `FINISHED`. ## Processing Receiving at KNAPP-Store The KNAPP-Store system has the capability to receive goods without advance notification. **Master Data Prerequisites:** For all pack units that have to be received, the following restrictions apply - `KNAPPSTORE_TREATABLE` flag in `articleFeatures`: + KiSoft One is WMS & no assignment of article to location: all pack units that have to be stored at a KNAPP-Store must have the `KNAPPSTORE_TREATABLE` flag set + KiSoft One is WMS & assignment of article to location: the flag `KNAPPSTORE_TREATABLE` has not be set + KiSoft One is WCS & assignment of article to location: the flag `KNAPPSTORE_TREATABLE` has not be set - pack units that are of a cylindrical shape must have `CYLINDRICAL` flag set in `articleFeatures` - all pack units must have at least one defined optical code that can identify the pack unit When receiving goods at KNAPP-Store, stock is created as the articles are scanned by the KNAPP-Store inbound scanner on a per item basis. ![Processing Receiving at KNAPP-Store](./img/KNAPP-Store_BlindReceiving.svg) Used Messages: [PostStockReceived](#operation/PostStockReceived), [PostStockMoved](#operation/PostStockMoved), [PostStockLockChanged](#operation/PostStockLockChanged) ## Crossdocking ### Single-stage crossdocking (CD1) ![Crossdocking](./img/crossdocking.svg) Used Messages: [PostCrossdockingOutboundDelivery](#operation/PostCrossdockingOutboundDelivery), [PostOutboundDeliveryReply](#operation/PostCrossdocking), [PostCrossdocking](#operation/PostCrossdocking), [PostCrossdockingReply](#operation/PostCrossdockingReply), [PatchCrossdocking](#operation/PatchCrossdocking), [DeleteCrossdocking](#operation/DeleteCrossdocking), [PatchOutboundDelivery](#operation/PatchOutboundDelivery), [PostOutboundDeliveryReply](#operation/PostOutboundDeliveryReply) In the single-stage crossdocking load units from goods in are fed directly to goods out. No internal warehouse processes are carried out and no stock is created. The relevant load units are linked to one outbound delivery each in the course of goods in processing. The corresponding outbound delivery must always be available in KiSoft One in advance. After receiving the goods-in load unit can be placed or booked to a transfer location or a structured load unit (e.g. pallet). This goods-in load units are then further processed by the goods-out process. ### Two-stage crossdocking (CD2) ![Crossdocking](./img/crossdocking2.svg) Used Messages: [PostInboundDelivery](#operation/PostInboundDelivery), [PostInboundDeliveryReply](#operation/PostInboundDeliveryReply), [PostOutboundDelivery](#operation/PostOutboundDelivery), [PostOutboundDeliveryReply](#operation/PostOutboundDeliveryReply) In the two-stage crossdocking articles are fed into the warehouse via goods in, put into storage and then picked via an outbound delivery and fed into goods out. The goods therefore flow through the warehouse. In order to be able to handle the flow of goods separately, articles must be marked with the `stockType` for two-stage crossdocking (e.g. `CD2`). The stock is assigned to a specific customer or a specific delivery. In the message [PostInboundDelivery](#operation/PostInboundDelivery) the project specific `stockType` for two-stage crossdocking (e.g. `CD2`) and also a `reservationCode` has to be transmitted. In the [PostOutboundDelivery](#operation/PostOutboundDelivery) message the project specific `stockType` for two-stage crossdocking (e.g. `CD2`) and also a `reservationCode` has to be transmitted (the same `reservationCode` as for the InboundDelivery). ## Direct Control of a Load Unit The higher-level systems can use the capabilities of KiSoft to control a load unit (unknown to KiSoft) through the warehouse. ![Direct Control](./img/directcontrol.svg) Used Messages: [PostDirectControl](#operation/PostDirectControl), [PostDirectControlReply](#operation/PostDirectControlReply)

tags.name: Storage Usecases
tags.description: Storage describes the process of bringing a load unit or article to a location or slot in the warehouse for the first time. ## Store a Storage Load Unit In order to store any load unit at a KiSoft managed storage area, the higher level system has to provide the necessary information by using the StorageOrder message. See [PostStorageOrder](#operation/PostStorageOrder) for details. **Prerequisites:** - The load unit provided must not be known to KiSoft - KiSoft has to be physically and logically able to control the specified load carrier from the hand over position to the designated storage area. As long as the StorageOrder is not startet at KiSoft, the order may be updated with new information. As soon as KiSoft started the order, KiSoft takes control of the generated stock, and stock messages are transmitted when changes occur to the stock. (e.g. stock corrections etc.). ![external stock taking](./img/general_StorageOrder.svg) Used Messages: [PostStorageOrder](#operation/PostStorageOrder), [PatchStorageOrder](#operation/PatchStorageOrder), [PostStorageOrderReply](#operation/PostStorageOrderReply) ## Storing Externally Received Stock and Tracking Stock Information Used Messages: [PostStorageOrder](#operation/PostStorageOrder) KiSoft can take over stock that was received externally and store it in the warehouse. **Prerequisites:** - The load unit provided must not be known to KiSoft - All stock information has to be provided by the higher-level system - The load unit has to be handed over to KiSoft at a location where KiSoft can take control of the load unit Upon receiving this message, KiSoft or its stock-keeping subsystems will automatically generate stock (no stock creation message is sent to the higher-level system in this case). As soon as the storageOrder reaches `processingStatus` = `"STARTED"`, stock change messages (e.g. [PostStockCorrected](#operation/PostStockCorrected)) may be sent by KiSoft for stock concerning this load unit. ## Storing a Storage Load Unit with Stock to OSR System Used Messages: [PostStorageOrder](#operation/PostStorageOrder) When a load unit has to be stored within an OSR system supplementary information has to be given by the higher-level system in order to enable KiSoft to handle the load unit accordingly. For a general use case and requirements therefore, see [Store a Storage Load Unit](#store-load-unit) and [Storing Externally Received Stock](#store-new-stock). **Master Data Prerequisites:** For all pack units that have to be stored, the following restrictions apply - pack units must have the `OSR_TREATABLE` flag set in `articleFeatures` - `capacityInformation` for stored articles have to be provided in order to use certain features of an OSR system. (Data can be provided either by master data or directly with the `loadUnitStock` full pack unit information). `possibleFinalTarget` can be used to store the load unit near a location where it will be needed in the future(e.g. for replenishing a flow rack). When load units are stored in an OSR system the stock gets available as soon as the storage order is `"FINISHED"`. This is the case when it reaches the OSR area. ## Request Route Assistance for a Storage Order Used Messages: [PostStorageOrder](#operation/PostStorageOrder) KiSoft can manage a multitude of entry points to warehouse system or storage subsystem. In order to assist the higher-level system in choosing the right entry point, the higher level system can send a storage order with `"ROUTE_ASSISTANCE"` flag added to the `controlFlags` property. After receiving the storage order with this flag set, KiSoft will immediately send a storage order reply message containing the `routeAssistance` property that contains a list of possible entry points (that can be used as `handoverPosition`) for the higher level system. ## Store a Load Unit within an OSR System that must not be used by KiSoft Used Messages: [PostStorageOrder](#operation/PostStorageOrder) The higher level system can store load units that must not be used by KiSoft by any means. In order to do that, a normal storage order is utilized but with the property `contentType` set to `"BLOCKED"`. The load unit will also not be considered for any housekeeping operations like merge or defrag (in case of empty slots). If stock information is transmitted for the load unit, this stock will not be considered for any stock reports or stock inventories of KiSoft. If some time later the load unit shall be used by KiSoft, a separate message ([PostChangeLoadUnitRequest](#operation/PostChangeLoadUnitRequest)) has to be sent to set the load unit to the appropriate `contentType` (e.g. `"STOCK"`). ## Store a Load Unit to Knapp-Store **Master Data Prerequisites:** For all pack units that have to be stored, the following restrictions apply - `KNAPPSTORE_TREATABLE` flag in `articleFeatures`: + KiSoft One is WMS & no assignment of article to location: all pack units that have to be stored at a KNAPP-Store must have the `KNAPPSTORE_TREATABLE` flag set + KiSoft One is WMS & assignment of article to location: the flag `KNAPPSTORE_TREATABLE` has not be set + KiSoft One is WCS & assignment of article to location: the flag `KNAPPSTORE_TREATABLE` has not be set - pack units that are of a cylindrical shape must have `CYLINDRICAL` flag set in `articleFeatures` - all pack units must have at least one defined optical code that can identify the pack unit For a more general use case and requirements therefore, see [Storing Externally Received Stock](#store-new-stock). As soon as the load unit is scanned at KNAPP-Store the storage order will be finished. The stock however may still take a little while until all items are available for picking (the time from the goods-in belt to the storage locations within KNAPP-Store). If the higher-level system requires more information (like when single items are available for picking), it has to consider the stock change messages that can be sent by KiSoft. ![Processing of Storage Orders for KNAPP-Store](./img/KNAPP-Store_StorageOrder.svg) Used Messages: [PostStorageOrder](#operation/PostStorageOrder), [PostStorageOrderReply](#operation/PostStorageOrderReply), [PostStockLockChanged](#operation/PostStockLockChanged), [PostStockMoved](#operation/PostStockMoved) ## Store (Replenishment) to Manual Stations Used Messages: [PostStorageOrder](#operation/PostStorageOrder) The higher-level system can utilize KiSoft to replenish locations at manual stations by storing load units at distinct locations. For a general use case and requirements therefore, see [Store a Storage Load Unit](#store-load-unit) In order to enable KiSoft to control the load unit to a specific location, `locationNumber` has to be set in the `targetPosition` property. After load unit reaches the target location, the order is `"FINISHED"`

tags.name: Relocation Usecases
tags.description:
Relocation describes the process of bringing a load unit or article from one location or slot to another location or slot within the warehouse.
Articles, filled load units, empty load units, etc. can be relocated.


**General Prerequisites:**
- KiSoft has to be able access and control the requested load units / articles to the new location by the for this warehouse installed means of transportation
- the target location has to be able to physically store the requested load units / articles

![General processing of a Relocation Request](./img/generalRelocationRequest.svg)

Used Messages: [PostRelocationRequestArticles](#operation/PostRelocationRequestArticles), [PostRelocationRequestLoadUnits](#operation/PostRelocationRequestLoadUnits), [PostRelocationRequestEmptyLoadUnits](#operation/PostRelocationRequestEmptyLoadUnits), [PostRelocationRequestReply](#operation/PostRelocationRequestReply), [PostLoadUnitMoved](#operation/PostLoadUnitMoved)

## Relocate a Number of Articles to a New Location

Used Messages: [PostRelocationRequestArticles](#operation/PostRelocationRequestArticles)

With `requestedItem` quantities of specific pack units (matching all specified stock properties) can be requested. KiSoft will allocate the requested stock and control it to the to the location specified by `targetPosition` using one or more load units.



## Relocate Specific Load Units to New Location

Used Messages: [PostRelocationRequestLoadUnits](#operation/PostRelocationRequestLoadUnits)

With `requestedItems` a number of load units can be requested and controlled to the location specified by `targetPosition`.



## Relocate a Number of Empty Load Units to a New Location

Used Messages: [PostRelocationRequestEmptyLoadUnits](#operation/PostRelocationRequestEmptyLoadUnits)

With `requestedAmount` a number of empty load units for a specific load carrier can be specified.
KiSoft will allocate the requested load units and control it to the to the location specified by `targetPosition` using one or more load units.

> **NOTE:** The value of the field `storageArea` in `targetPosition` can also specify a workstation, in case the empty load units have to be controlled to a specific workstation.

tags.name: Inventory Usecases
tags.description: When KiSoft or one of its subsystems have to manage stock quantities, KiSoft will notify the higher-level system if unexpected changes occur. Additionally KiSoft can provide means to do inventory and report stock levels. # Inventory During inventory, the expected stock, which is the quantity that should be present at a location (for example, in a load unit) according to the stock management system, is compared with the actual stock, which is the quantity that is actually there at the location being inventoried. If the actual stock deviates from the expected stock, these deviations are compared in the stock management system (warehouse operating company system or KiSoft) and, if necessary, supplied to other systems. ## Request Inventory The inventory request can be used to do the following types of inventory processes: * location based inventory → only location information (`locationNumber`) is provided in `inventoryRequestLine` * load unit based inventory → only load unit information (`loadUnitCode`)is provided in `inventoryRequestLine` * article based inventory → any other information or combination of information is provided in `inventoryRequestLine` (at least `articleNumber`) Which type of inventory is carried out, depends solely on the information provided in `inventoryRequestLine`. If the parts of the stock requested is currently handled by a subsystem, KiSoft will forward the inventory request in a format appropriate to the subsystem. ![General Processing of an Inventory Request](./img/generalInventoryRequest.svg) Used Messages: [PostInventoryRequest](#operation/PostInventoryRequest), [PostStockCorrected](#operation/PostStockCorrected), [PostInventoryRequestReply](#operation/PostInventoryRequestReply) ## Specific Inventories Specific inventory orders can be created by sending a separate `businessCase` for the inventory request. Possible non standard values of `businessCase` for inventory have to be determined during the planning phase of the project. Specific inventory orders are not queued with the general inventory orders but are placed in a separate queue. This make it possible to process special inventory orders separately of general inventory orders. At the work stations, the user switches to the specific inventory mode to process these specific inventory orders. ## KNAPP-Store Specifics If stock is located at a KNAPP-Store, the system handles the inventory of the articles automatically by checking if all in question are still at their appointed positions. # Stock Stock is a general technical term for the goods that are held in supply in a warehouse. At KiSoft stock is managed separately for each defined quantity of the same pack units of an article whereby all pack units have the same stock separating properties and are together at one place. ## Stock Level Relevant Messages When stock is booked in KiSoft, this is **always** due to a specific reason with a specific action. Changes to the stock object are only reported to the higher-level system if the change occurs outside of regular warehouse processes (picking, replenishment etc.) The following messages may be triggered by changes in stock data: | Message OperationID | Reason | Remarks | | ----------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `postStockReceived` | N/A | Stock levels are increased, since new stock was created. | | `postStockCorrected` | `"PICK_ERROR"`, `"QUANTITY_DIFFERENCE"`, `"LOST"` | The physical quantity was changed due to missing or otherwise changed number of items | | `postStockLockChanged` | N/A | The logically available quantity may be increased or decreased because of locking unlocking stock. | | `postStockChanged` | N/A | Informs the higher level system that fundamental stock properties have changed for some stock. This may result in different stock levels for the higher-level system depending its configuration of stock separating properties. | | `postStockMoved` | N/A | Typically this message does not change overall stock levels, but tells the higher-level system that stock was moved to another location or load unit. | | `postLoadUnitSlotEmpty` | N/A | This message is somewhat an outlier and does not count to the "stock relevant messages" nevertheless for completeness reasons and because it is sometimes used by higher-level systems it is listed here as it is sent when a load unit was confirmed being empty | | Reasons | Meaning | | -------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `HOST` | Action has been triggered by the higher-level system | | `SYSTEM` | KiSoft triggered the stock change. | | `SUBSYSTEM` | A subsystem (OSR, OS-Store, KNAPP-Store etc.) has created or changed the stock. | | `LOST` | Stock can no longer be found for unspecified reasons. | | `PICK_ERROR` | Stock has been removed after failed picking. | | `QUANTITY_DIFFERENCE` | A stock difference has been detected during inventory | | `AUTO_COUNT_UNTOUCHED_LOU` | Reason when booking for inventory: Automatic counting of non-processed load units. | ### Stock Lifecycle in KiSoft One The life of stock starts when it is created (e.g. at goods-in) and maintained until the quantity of that particular stock reaches zero. When partial quantities are withdrawn from stock, new stock is created and tracked separately for the withdrawn quantity. ## Maintaining 4 Wall Stock Levels During Operation This use case displays how the higher-level system shall track stock levels during warehouse operation. > **Important!:** KiSoft books the stock out at the time the **goods-out order** is `"FINISHED"`. > > If the higher-level system uses the outbound delivery reply to book the stock, a storage report ([`PostStorageReport`](#operation/PostStorageReport)) taken in the time between the **goods-out order** is `"FINISHED"` and the corresponding **outbound delivery** is `"FINISHED"` results in a mismatch of stock levels (since KiSoft already booked the stock out and therefore the stock is missing in the snapshot) but the higher-level system has not (since the outbound delivery is still active). ![Stock Relevant Messages](./img/4WallStockInOperation.svg) Used Messages: [PostStockReceived](#operation/PostStockReceived), [PostStockCorrected](#operation/PostStockCorrected), [PostGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrderReply), [PostOutboundDeliveryReply](#operation/PostOutboundDeliveryReply) ## Stock Corrections Used Messages: [`PostStockCorrected`](#operation/PostStockCorrected) Whenever the actual stock deviates from the expected stock, these deviations are compared in KiSoft and a stock correction message sent to the higher-level system. ## Locking Stock When stock is locked, picking for outbound processes is prohibited. Stock can be locked and unlocked using the [`PostStockLockRequest` message](#operation/PostStockLockRequest). When locking unlocking stock, this can be done for a multitude of stock objects. Depending on the amount of affected stock objects, the operation can take some time. For all changed stock objects, a notification is sent to the higher-level system. ![Lock Stock](./img/stockLock.svg) Used Messages: [PostStockLockRequest](#operation/PostStockLockRequest), [PostStockLockChanged](#operation/PostStockLockChanged) ## Change Stock Properties of a Load Unit In case of a systematic error, or when in need to change certain stock properties, the higher-level system can use the [PostChangeLoadUnitRequest](#operation/PostChangeLoadUnitRequest) to request the alteration of specific stock properties of a load unit. Since this message affect a change of the warehouse stock, caution has to be applied when using this message. The higher level system can change stock (e.g. of a certain lot) by setting the respective properties in `changeLoadUnitRequest`. KiSoft will search and process the matching stock of the load unit and change the properties. No stock change messages will be sent to the higher level system. ![Change Stock Properties](./img/stockPropertyChange.svg) Used Messages: [PostChangeLoadUnitRequest](#operation/PostChangeLoadUnitRequest) ## Decant or Merging or Moving Stock When stock of two separated places (different location or load unit slots) are merged together or stock is repacked to a different pack unit or stock is moved, a [PostStockMoved](#operation/PostStockMoved) message is sent to the higher-level system. It is possible, that the source load unit becomes empty or that there is some stock left. ![Merge Stock of two Load Units](./img/stockMerge.svg) Used Messages: [PostStockMoved](#operation/PostStockMoved), [PostLoadUnitSlotEmpty](#operation/PostLoadUnitSlotEmpty) ## Load Unit Slot got Empty In order to enable the higher-level system to keep track of empty load units / empty slots KiSoft sends [PostLoadUnitSlotEmpty](#operation/PostLoadUnitSlotEmpty) to the higher level system. If the whole load unit was emptied after an operation the value of `isLoadUnitEmpty` is set to `true`.

tags.name: Goods-Out Usecases
tags.description: Goods-Out describes the processing of warehouse orders used to move the articles planned for an outbound delivery or part of those articles from their locations to goods-out. During planning, in the case that KiSoft has to split outbound deliveries, KiSoft generates one or more goods-out orders from an outbound delivery (as many goods-out orders as necessary to process the outbound delivery). A goods-out order pertains to 1 load unit, i.e. with a goods-out order, 1 load unit is moved to goods-out. Note, that the information given in use cases can be combined in order to accomplish more complex scenarios. ## General A goods-out order includes all warehouse tasks necessary to move the articles of the goods-out order from their location to goods-out. Examples of warehouse tasks in a goods-out order: retrieve, pick, check, pack, lid. Depending on the tasks that have to be processed, different information has to be provided in the goods-out order message. ![General Goods-Out Order Processing](./img/general_goodsOutOrder.svg) Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder), [PatchGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PatchGoodsOutOrder), [PostGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrderReply) ## Goods-out Additional Processing at Target Workstations Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder) Mandatory data fields for this use case: - `transportTargets` Goods-out orders can be directed to multiple target workstations by adding workstations in the field `transportTargets` to the request. As soon as a load unit arrives and can enter (divert to) the requested station, the transport task to that station is considered finished. For all visited workstations transmitted in `transportTargets`, a reply message [`postGoodsOutOrderReply`](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrderReply) will be sent to the higher-level system. ## Goods-out Picking of Lines The most common use of goods-out orders is to let KiSoft pick the items that are requested via the goods-out order. In order to do this the higher-level system has to transmit an order line for each article that has to be picked. KiSoft will determine, based on the information presented in the order line, where to pick the items. Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder) Mandatory data fields for this use case: - `goodsOutOrderLines` ## Goods-out with Additional Processing Requirements Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder) Processing requirements for the whole goods-out order are transmitted via the `controlFlags` property. If a load unit has to be e.g. lidded and strapped before shipping, the corresponding values (`"LID"` and `"STRAP"` for our example) have to be set for the goods-out order. ## Value Added Services Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder) If value added services are required for an goods-out order or outbound delivery, the `vasTasks` property has to be transmitted. This data field enables the transmission of multiple VAS activities that have to be processed. `workZone` information is used to indicate to KiSoft where the task shall be processed. `lineReference` links the VAS activities to specific order lines. Without line reference, the transmitted VAS task applies to the complete order. ## Buffering and Retrieval of Goods-Out Orders Buffering of goods-out orders can be used for many purposes. The following two are just examples: - When articles for an goods-out order are pre-picked to avoid load peaks within the warehouse it is often necessary to store the resulting load units until they are needed. - Another uses case can be that not all articles are available for picking when the order is transmitted but the higher-level system wants to pick whats possible and get the rest when it was replenished. Therefore order is picked and temporarily stored for subsequent picking of the remaining articles. In order to buffer goods-out orders, the higher-level system has to transmit the value `"BUFFER"` in the field `controlFlags`. KiSoft will process the goods-out order and store it in the buffering system. After the goods-out order was stored in the buffer system, the order waits until it is relocated again by the higher-level system. After the higher-level system has requested the relocation for the load unit, processing of the goods-out order will continue. ![Buffering and Retrieving a Goods-Out Order](./img/buffering_goodsOutOrder.svg) Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder), [PostGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrderReply), [PostRelocationRequestLoadUnits](#operation/PostRelocationRequestLoadUnits), [PostRelocationRequestReply](#operation/PostRelocationRequestReply) ## Goods-out Orders Sequenced To Dispatch Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder), [PostRelocationRequestLoadUnits](#operation/PostRelocationRequestLoadUnits) In order to bring goods-out orders sequenced to the dispatch area, buffering those orders is necessary (see [Buffering and Retrieval of Goods-Out Orders](#buffering-goods-out)). The higher-level system can bring any amount of load units into a sequence, by transmitting a single relocation request for all load units in question, and using the field `sequence` in order to bring those load units into a distinct order. ## Batch-picking And Sortation > In this scenario, batch building is done by the higher-level system. > It is not possible to update a batched goods-out order via an update message. If such an order needs to be changed, the order has to be deleted ([DeleteBatchedGoodsOutOrder](#operation/DeleteBatchedGoodsOutOrder)) and transmitted again ([PostBatchedGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostBatchedGoodsOutOrder)). In order to utilize the KNAPP products Sort and Pack or Pack 2 Patient a different `businessCase` is used. Since the order is not shipped, in the form it is initially picked, the businessCase `"PREPICK"` is used. The prepick order is used to acquire all necessary articles. After all articles are picked, they will be transported to a workstation, where sortation can be done. After the last item was sorted, the "PREPICK" order is finished. The sorted items are then picked into new system generated goods-out orders (with `businessCase`=`"GOODS_OUT"` that are referencing the original prepick order) and finally shipped. Since the same articles may be sorted for different targets, the order lines of the prepick order may contain multiple lines with the same article but with different sort criteria (`batchSortCriterion`). > Usually `batchSortCriterion` contains the id of the patient order (e.g. for Pack 2 Patient sorting systems) to which also print data may be linked. ![Batch picking flow chart](./img/prepickGoodsOutOrder.svg) Used Messages: [PostBatchedGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostBatchedGoodsOutOrder), [PostBatchGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostBatchGoodsOutOrderReply), [PostGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrderReply), [PostStructuredGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostStructuredGoodsOutOrderReply) **Example for Process of Pack to Patient (P2P)** ![Pack to Patient](./img/packtopatient.svg) For the Pack 2 Patient use case the higher-level system has to send the message [PostBatchedGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostBatchedGoodsOutOrder) with the corresponding order number (batched order has no sheetNumber). The load unit(s) for this order contain(s) the articles for the patients of this order. After this batched order gets sorted, KiSoft One generates goods-out orders with the order number of the batched order and sheet numbers and also additional load units for shipping. When this goods-out orders are finished, KiSoft One sends the return message [PostGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrderReply) for each generated goods-out order (with processingStatus `"FINISHED"`). When the bags are packed into a carton for shipping, the structured goods-out order gets finished and KiSoft One sends the return message [PostStructuredGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostStructuredGoodsOutOrderReply) (with processingStatus `"FINISHED"`). When the structured goods-out order is finished, the batched goods-out order gets finished and KiSoft One sends the return message [PostBatchGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostBatchGoodsOutOrderReply) (with processingStatus `"FINISHED"`). # External Processing of Goods-Out Orders The term "external processing" means that parts of the order is processed outside of KiSoft or one of its subsystems. In order to enable KiSoft to track tasks that were transmitted by the higher-level system, that are processed outside of KiSoft, the higher-level system can transmit the processing results to KiSoft via [PostOrderProcessingExternal](#operation/PostOrderProcessingExternal). With this message, the higher-level system acts as a 'subsystem' of KiSoft by providing processing information to certain orders. ## Picking Lines Externally When picking or order lines is done by the higher-level system but KiSoft has to keep track of the order lines being processed, the higher-level system has to transmit the processing information of the `processedGoodsOutOrderLines` via [PostOrderProcessingExternal](#operation/PostOrderProcessingExternal) used data fields for goods-out order: - `goodsOutOrderLines` used data fields for external processing: - `processedGoodsOutOrderLines` The higher-level system has to send the externally processed quantity in the attribute `processedQuantity`. ![External Order Processing](./img/externalOrderProcessing.svg) Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder), [PostOrderProcessingExternal](#operation/PostOrderProcessingExternal), [PostGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrderReply) ## Goods-out Externally Tracked Picking at Target Stations It might be necessary for the higher-level system require an order to be processed by the higher-level system itself at certain workstations and have KiSoft divert the order to those workstations as long as the higher-level system has confirmed the processing to KiSoft. In order to achieve this, the target stations concerned are configured in a way, so that arriving load units at the workstation are not automatically counting as processed. The confirmation that a station was processed correctly has to be transmitted by the higher-level system via [PostOrderProcessingExternal](#operation/PostOrderProcessingExternal) with `processedGoodsOutOrderLines` and `processingCode` = `"OK"`. Used Messages: [PostGoodsOutOrder](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrder), [PostOrderProcessingExternal](#operation/PostOrderProcessingExternal) used data fields for goods-out order: - `transportTargets` used data fields for external processing: - `processedGoodsOutOrderLines` ## External Processing Movement of a Load Unit The higher-level system moves a load unit externally to a station. Used Messages: [PostLoadUnitMovedExternal](#operation/PostLoadUnitMovedExternal) ![External Order Processing](./img/loadUnitMovedExternal.svg)

tags.name: Outbound Deliveries Usecases
tags.description: Outbound deliveries process a quantity of one or more articles which, because of one or more customer purchase orders, is delivered to a customer. During planning, an outbound delivery is created from a customer purchase order by the higher-level system. Stock control is also carried out (is there sufficient stock in the warehouse to process the customer purchase order?). Furthermore, the specific articles and quantities are specified that belong to the outbound delivery. If there is insufficient stock available in the warehouse for a certain article, the outbound delivery usually includes a lesser quantity than the purchase order. In the following sections, some common usecases regarding outbound delivery handling are presented. ## Outbound Delivery For the received outbound delivery, KiSoft will create as many goods-out orders as are needed to fulfill the outbound delivery. > **Note:** Goods-out orders that were created by KiSoft (orders where the value of `createdBy` equals `SYSTEM` ) can not be updated by the higher-level system via `updateGoodsOutOrder`. Any requests to such orders are rejected. ![Outbound Delivery Sample Flow Diagram](./img/outboundDelivery.svg) Used Messages: [PostOutboundDelivery](#operation/PostOutboundDelivery), [PatchOutboundDelivery](#operation/PatchOutboundDelivery), [PostOutboundDeliveryReply](#operation/PostOutboundDeliveryReply), [PostGoodsOutOrderReply](#operation/PostGoodsOutOrderReply) ## Value Added Services Used Messages: [PostOutboundDelivery](#operation/PostOutboundDelivery) Value added services can be required for an outbound delivery via the `vasTasks` property. This data field enables the transmission of multiple VAS activities that have to be processed. `workZone` information is used to indicate to KiSoft where the task shall be processed. `lineReference` links the VAS activities to specific order lines. Without line reference, the transmitted VAS task applies to all created goods-out orders. ## Crossdocking See Inbound Use Cases: [Use case Crossdocking](#crossdocking)

tags.name: General Usecases
tags.description: The following sections describe scenarios and principles that are either applied throughout all logistical processes or are not linked to logistical processes at all. ## Business Cases The business case defines how a request or order is processed and handled within the warehouse. Warehouses might have different business cases defined for e.g. bringing goods to the dispatch area, because some orders have to fulfill other requirements than others. Example: The higher-level system wants KiSoft to handle oversized articles differently. This can be achieved by defining an distinct value for `businessCase` like `OVERSIZE` and define during the planning phase of the project, what orders of that business case have to do. E.g. the orders shall only be processed by RF and pallet picking. The following standard values are defined: - `"GOODS_IN"` - `"GOODS_OUT"` - `"INVENTORY"` - `"MERGE"` - `"MOVEMENT"` - `"PREPICK"` - `"RELOCATION"` - `"REPLENISHMENT"` - `"TRANSPORT"` # Standard Reply Schema A warehouse produces during operation a multitude of notifications with valuable information. In this section we describe our standard for handling respond messages to the higher-level system. The feedback is standardised, no individual feedback messages to the higher-level system are provided. Some reply messages can be enabled or disabled by global settings: - KiSoft generated storageOrders - externally generated storageOrders - corrections of stock - changes of stock locks - changes of stock attributes - load unit slot got empty Additionally workstation specific reply messages can be configured per message type (workstation and station action can be configured): - outbound delivery and goods-out order: station reply message with line informations - moving of load unit: load unit moved in or out a workstation, optional also movement of empty load units can be reported - moving of stock decant, merge, defrag Further details for respond messages can be enabled or disabled, depending on the notification and the trigger event. The details can be found in the corresponding response messages.

tags.name: Appendix
tags.description: ## Return Codes The following return codes can be sent to the higher level system: | Code | Description | |---|---| | 200 | Processing of message was successful. | | 207 | Some operations failed. | | 400 | A bad request has been sent. | | 409 | Operation is not allowed. | Also the standard HTTP response status codes (e. g. 500) can be sent to the higher level system. For further detail see https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Status#server_error_responses. ## Error Response Codes The error numbers are built upon this schema: - Severity (E): + Error - System (AKO): + Knapp AG (A) + KiSoft One (KO) - Module (XXXX): + General (GENR) + Masterdata (MAST) + Movementdata (MOVM) + Stock-Management (STOC) The following error codes can be sent to the higher level system: | Code | Description | |---|---| | E-AKO-GENR-0001 | General error | | E-AKO-GENR-0002 | Format error | | E-AKO-MAST-0001 | Unknown article | | E-AKO-MAST-0002 | Unknown customer | | E-AKO-MAST-0003 | Unknown route | | E-AKO-MAST-0004 | Unknown supplier | | E-AKO-MAST-0005 | Unknown ramp number | | E-AKO-MAST-0006 | Wrong pack size | | E-AKO-MAST-0007 | No departure time | | E-AKO-MAST-0008 | Route departure time < route ramp occupation time is not allowed | | E-AKO-MAST-0009 | Unknown location | | E-AKO-MAST-0010 | Invalid overstock location code for article | | E-AKO-MAST-0011 | Wrong product eject code | | E-AKO-MAST-0012 | Wrong pack unit height dimension | | E-AKO-MAST-0013 | Wrong pack unit length dimension | | E-AKO-MAST-0014 | There is already an article on this location, but only one article is allowed | | E-AKO-MAST-0015 | Unknown storage area or station name | | E-AKO-MOVM-0001 | Invalid order number | | E-AKO-MOVM-0002 | An order with orderNumber/sheetNumber is active | | E-AKO-MOVM-0003 | Order number not found | | E-AKO-MOVM-0004 | CD1 unknown outbound delivery | | E-AKO-MOVM-0005 | Wrong order process status | | E-AKO-MOVM-0006 | Line not found (usually when trying to delete line in order-update) | | E-AKO-MOVM-0007 | Unknown sheet number | | E-AKO-MOVM-0008 | Order has a wrong start station | | E-AKO-MOVM-0009 | Wrong minimum, maximum or expected order weight | | E-AKO-MOVM-0010 | Wrong business case | | E-AKO-MOVM-0011 | An order with this load unit number is already active | | E-AKO-MOVM-0012 | Planning strategy does not exist | | E-AKO-STOC-0001 | Not enough stock available | | E-AKO-STOC-0002 | Active stock-record exists. Not allowed to delete referenced article or supplier | | E-AKO-STOC-0003 | Unknown load unit | | E-AKO-STOC-0005 | Storage unit already exists | | E-AKO-STOC-0006 | Load unit is currently in use for replenishment and can not be modified | ## Example XML Message Example for message POST inboundDelivery: ```XML EXPIRED QS_REQUIRED HOST QS_REQUIRED ```

put.summary: Create or Update Pack Unit Information
put.description:
`operationId: PutPackUnits`



Pack units (PU) are managed for each article and represent the different quantities (pack sizes given by `packSize`) of jointly packed units of an article.

If several pack units already exist for an article, transferred properties of the `article` entity are adopted for all existing pack units. This means that the last information sent in the `article` entity is used for all linked pack units.

The `packSize` for an article has to be unique. That means that for an article only one pack unit of a specific `packSize` can be defined.

KiSoft one needs information for at least one pack unit for any article in order to be able to process the article.
If pack units are managed in KiSoft One (meaning that pack units are created at KiSoft during goods-in), the smallest pack unit (usually with pack size "1", but can be any pack size) has to be transferred for this article.



The following operations are possible with this message:
- Create a new pack unit for a new article
- Create a new pack unit for an already known article
- Change properties of an already known pack unit and / or article

post.description:
`operationId: PostBatchedGoodsOutOrder`

Batched goods-out orders are used when the higher-level system takes the planning responsibility and determines what articles have to be picked together in one load unit.
Batched goods-out orders are also used for Pack 2 Patient (P2P).

In this special case, the higher level system has batched together more than one orders in order to enable efficient picking.
After picking, batch orders are sorted and packed before the final orders are shipped.

KiSoft will process the load unit and report the results to the higher-level system.

The quantity to be picked is the difference between requestedQuantity and providedQuantity.

post.description:
`operationId: PostInboundDelivery`

An inbound delivery is a quantity of one or more articles which, because of one or more supplier purchase orders or because of one or more customer returns, is delivered to a warehouse operating company.

With the inbound delivery message, KiSoft One is informed that goods are inbound to the warehouse system
(e.g. trucks are unloaded at a delivery ramp and the pallets are parked near goods-in work stations) and will be available for storage in the KNAPP system.

> **NOTE:**
> Article masterdata for all articles have to be present in advance.
> `packSize` is a required field for inbound deliveries. If the pack size is not known to the higher-level system, `"1"` can be transmitted. The pack unit with the correct pack size is then determined during stock taking.

For a sample use case see: [Use case goods-in processing](#goods-in-processing)

post.summary: External processing movement of a Load Unit
post.description:
`operationId: PostLoadUnitMovedExternal`

This message provides means to communicate relevant movement information by an external (not KiSoft) system for a load unit actually in processing by KiSoft.

This might be useful when a load unit leaves (or gets removed) the KiSoft controlled parts of the system.
Furthermore this message might be utilized for unregistering a load unit at KiSoft using specified EXIT positions.

post.description:
`operationId: PostDirectControl`

This message can be used to pass a load unit (unknown to KiSoft) through the KiSoft managed warehouse (e.g. the higher level system wants to use that transport
capabilities of the KiSoft warehouse for its own purpose). This message is intended to allow the host to control a container through the KiSoft managed warehouse.

The transport has to target a known position (that is no storage location) within the KiSoft managed warehouse.

post.description:
`operationId: PostRequestWorkStationStatus`

This message can be used to get the status of workstations. If stationName is transmitted, only the status for this station is returned.
If no stationName is transmitted, all stations are returned.
The response for this message is the message [PostWorkStationStatus](#operation/PostWorkStationStatus).
