post.description:
`operationId: PostHeartbeat`

This message provides the heartbeat functionality as described in [Websocket Response Channel](#websocket)

`operationId: PostWorkStationStatus`

This message provides the information about the status and the warehouse tasks of stations. The warehouse tasks are sent, when a station is active.

This message can be sent because of the following two reasons:
- A station is configured and the station is activated or a warehouse task for the station is selected or changed. Then only this station is returned.
- The message is sent as response to the message [requestWorkStationStatus](#operation/PostRequestWorkStationStatus).

`operationId: PostInboundDeliveryReply`

Provides new processing information for an existing inbound delivery.

Possible values for `processingStatus` for `inboundDelivery` are:
  - `"NEW""`
  - `"STARTED"`
  - `"FINISHED"`
  - `"CANCELLED"`

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the `processingStatus` changes to one of the following values:
  - `"STARTED"`
  - `"FINISHED"`
  - `"CANCELLED"`

Line processing results  `inboundDeliveryLines` will only be transmitted for finished or canceled inbound deliveries.
For a sample use case see: [Use case goods-in processing](#goods-in-processing)

`operationId: PostStorageOrderReply`

This message is the response for a storage order and provides new information about an existing storage order.

Storage orders may be created by KiSoft during goods-in. Processing information will be sent to the higher-level system when the `processingStatus` changes.
Possible values for `processingStatus` for `storageOrder` are:
  - `"NEW"`
  - `"STARTED"`
  - `"FINISHED"`
  - `"CANCELLED"`

It can be configured, if this message will be sent.

`operationId: PostCrossdockingReply`

Provides new processing information for an existing crossdocking.

Possible values for `processingStatus` for `crossdocking` are:
  - `"NEW""`
  - `"STARTED"`
  - `"FINISHED"`
  - `"CANCELLED"`

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the `processingStatus` changes to one of the following values:
  - `"STARTED"`
  - `"FINISHED"`
  - `"CANCELLED"`

For sample use case see [Use case crossdocking](#crossdocking).

`operationId: PostOutboundDeliveryReply`

Provides new processing information for an existing outbound delivery.

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the `processingStatus` changes to one of the following values:
  - `"RELEASE_READY"`
  - `"STARTED"`
  - `"PROCESSED"`
  - `"FINISHED"`
  - `"CANCELLED"`

`operationId: PostGoodsOutOrderReply`

Provides new processing information for an existing goods-out order.

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when one of the following trigger events occur:
  - `processingStatus` changes to one of the following values:
    - `"NEW"` (only for KiSoft created orders; `createdBy` = `"SYSTEM"` or `"USER"`)
    - `"STARTED"`
    - `"PROCESSED"`
    - `"FINISHED"`
    - `"CANCELLED"`
    - `"RESTARTED"`
    - `"REMARRIED"`
  - a change in the `processingResult` of one of the transmitted `transportTargets`
  - active order gets linked to a new load unit (remarried)
  - reading the load unit at a station configured for station reply

`operationId: PostBatchedGoodsOutOrderReply`

Provides new processing information for an existing batch goods-out order.

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when one of the following trigger events occur:
  - `processingStatus` changes to one of the following values:
    - `"NEW"` (only for KiSoft created orders; `createdBy` = `"SYSTEM"` or `"USER"`)
    - `"STARTED"`
    - `"PROCESSED"`
    - `"FINISHED"`
    - `"CANCELLED"`
    - `"REMARRIED"`
  - active order gets linked to a new load unit (remarried)
  - reading the load unit at a station configured for station reply

`operationId: PostStructuredGoodsOutOrderReply`

Provides new processing information for an existing structured goods-out order for Pack to Patient (P2P).

This message contains the data of the structured load unit (carton) and also the bags per patient.

`operationId: PostInventoryRequestReply`

Provides new processing information for an existing inventory request
Possible values for `processingStatus` are:
  - `"CANCELLED"`
  - `"FINISHED"`
  - `"STARTED"`

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the `processingStatus` changes to one of the following values:
  - `"CANCELLED"`
  - `"FINISHED"`
  - `"STARTED"`

Line processing result  `inventoryRequestLine` will only be transmitted for finished requests. If a request was canceled, the processed line will not be transmitted.

> **NOTE:** This message must not be used to correct stock quantities of the higher-level system. Quantities transmitted in this message, merely summaries the actually counted quantities, hence the work done by the operators, subsystems and such. Quantities may already be incorrect at the time of transmission of this message. Instead the stock correction messages have to be applied accordingly.

`operationId: PostStorageReport`

Provides the requested storage report.

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the storage report was processed.

`operationId: PostInventoryReport`

Provides the requested inventory report.

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the inventory report was processed.

`operationId: PostLoadUnitReport`

Provides the requested stock report.

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the load unit report was processed.

`operationId: PostStorageCapacityReport`

The storage capacity report provides detailed information about available storage capacities.

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the storage capacity report was processed.

`operationId: PostSlotCapacityReport`

The slot capacity report provides detailed information about available storage capacities per slot type.

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when the slot capacity report was processed.

`operationId: PostStockReceived`

This message will be sent because stock was received at goods-in.
This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available),

`operationId: PostStockCorrected`

This notification will be sent, whenever the system detects an unexpected change in quantities.
It can be configured, if this message will be sent.

`operationId: PostStockChanged`

This notification is sent, whenever stock separating properties (except lock reasons) have changed.
It can be configured, if this message will be sent.

`operationId: PostStockMoved`

This notification is sent, whenever stock was moved from a location/slot to a different location/slot without changing overall quantities.
This message will also be sent, when stock was repacked to a different pack unit.
A workstation can be configured to send this reply message to the higher-level system.

`operationId: PostStockLockChanged`

This notification is sent, whenever a lock reason was added or removed for a stock object.
It can be configured, if this message will be sent.

`operationId: PostLoadUnitMoved`

This message notifies the higher-level system of the change of location of a specific load unit. The message may provide information about the content (if any) of the load unit and the current location it was put on. When and how often this message will be sent for a load unit can be configured.

Possible options for configuration:
- `"NONEMPTY"`:  all movements of nonempty load units in configured workstations are reported (entering/leaving the workstation)
- `"EMPTY"`: also movements of empty load units that change to/from workstations are reported
- `"ALL-TRANSFERS"`: also movements between locations within a configured workstation are reported

`operationId: PostDirectControlReply`

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when one of the following trigger events occur:
  - `processingStatus` changes to one of the following values:
    - `"NEW"` (only for KiSoft created orders; `createdBy` = `"SYSTEM"` or `"USER"`)
    - `"STARTED"`
    - `"FINISHED"`
    - `"CANCELLED"`

`operationId: PostLoadUnitSlotEmpty`

This message notifies the higher-level system,  when a slot in a specific load unit got empty. The message may provide information about the current location.
It can be configured, if this message will be sent.

`operationId: PostRelocationRequestReply`

This message is always sent to the higher-level system (no configuration available), when one of the following trigger events occur:
  - `processingStatus` changes to one of the following values:
    - `"STARTED"`
    - `"PROCESSED"`
    - `"FINISHED"`
    - `"CANCELLED"`

basic_user_authentication.description:
Basic authentication is a simple authentication scheme built into the HTTP protocol.
The client sends HTTP requests with the Authorization header that contains the word Basic word followed by a space and a base64-encoded string username:password

CommonLineCodesResponseBody.description: List of all error or status codes applicable to lines of the received message.

lineCode.description: Error code applicable to a specific line.

ErrorCodes.description: List of all error or status codes applicable to the received message.

Aisle.description:
Storage location-free area in the middle of a rack line system, between rack line systems or between rack lines.
The aisle is a travel path for warehouse workers or transporters (shuttles, stacker cranes etc.) for storing and retrieving the load units and goods.

Rack.description: A frame that stands on the floor or is fastened to the wall with partitions for the storage of objects.

UserCode.description: Operator or system that has done the processing.

OrderNumber.description:
Identifier assigned to an order. This information will be used as a general reference when communicating with the higher-level system.
<details>

The order number is normally assigned by the system that generates the order. Various systems generate orders depending on the order type.
A warehouse order includes all warehouse tasks that are necessary to process the warehouse order.
</details>

SheetNumber.description: Expansion of the order number that are assigned e.g. to individual picking orders when they are generated.

RequestNumber.description:
Identifier assigned to a request. This information will be used as a general reference when communicating with the higher-level system.
<details>

The request number is normally assigned by the system that generates the request. Various systems generate requests depending on the request type.
</details>

LineReference.description: Reference number which identifies the line. This value has to be unique for the request/order lines.

NoteOnProcessing.description: Provides any useful information for the operator. Will be displayed during processing.

VasProcessingNote.description: Provides any useful information for the operator for a vasTask. Will be displayed during processing.

BusinessCase.description:
Identifies the business case that should be applied to the message.
The business case defines how a request or order is processed and handled within the warehouse.

KiSoft defines standard business cases for common logistical processes (see [Business Cases](#business-cases) in General section).
`businessCase` can be omitted if only the standard (default value) is used for a message.

RequestedQuantity.description:
Number of single items (equals one piece of an item of `packSize`= 1) of an article that *shall* be processed (picked, received, checked, etc.)
<details>

The number of requested pack units is derived from the requested quantity divided by the pack size. Remainder will be treated as single items.
</details>

CurrentQuantity.description: Number of single items (equals one piece of an item of `packSize`= 1) of an article that *is currently* (at the time the data was taken) present.

ProcessedQuantity.description: Number of single items (equals one piece of an item of `packSize`= 1) of an article that *has been* processed (picked, received, checked, moved, etc.)

RejectedQuantity.description:
Number of single items (equals one piece of an item of `packSize`= 1) of an article that *has been* processed but was rejected because the article was deemed
unfit for processing (e.g. broken goods). This items are not received.

DeltaQuantity.description:
States the relative change for a quantity that was processed.
For stock quantities, this states the difference between the expected quantity and the verified quantity.

TaskProcessingResult.description:
States the processing status of a task
<details>

The following status values are possible:
  | Value    | Description                                   |
  |----------|-----------------------------------------------|
  | `"OPEN"`  | Task was not yet processed. |
  | `"FINISHED"` | Task was fulfilled without errors or with all errors remedied during processing.     |
  | `"CANCELLED"`  | Task was canceled |
  </details>

OutboundDeliveryLineProcessingResult.description:
States the processing status of an order line
The following status values are possible: |
Value
| Line was / will be processed |Description | |----------|--------------------|---------------------------| | `"UNTOUCHED"` | yes | Line was not processed. | | `"SPLIT"` | yes |Line was split successfully | | `"PROCESSED"` | yes |The requested quantity was fulfilled without errors or with all errors remedied during processing. | | `"QUANTITY_UNTRUSTED"`| yes | Technical errors occurred during processing. The actual processed quantity **might not** match the requested quantity. The transmitted quantity represents the best known processed quantity but the number might be incorrect.| | `"QUANTITY_ERROR"` | yes |Processing was not successful. The actual processed quantity **does not** match the requested quantity. The transmitted quantity represents the actually processed quantity. | | `"COUNT_ERROR"` | yes |The quantity was counted and corrected at a check station to a quantity other than the requested quantity. The transmitted quantity is the final processed quantity for the outbound delivery. | | `"UNKNOWN_ARTICLE"` | no | The `articleNumber` transmitted is unknown. The line will not be processed | | `"ZERO_REQUESTED"` | no | The `requestedQuantity` transmitted was zero | | `"MAX_PICK_QUANTITY"` | no | The line exceeded the maximum pick quantity. The transmitted quantity represents the actually processed quantity.| | `"LOAD_UNIT_FULL"`| no | The load unit got full during processing, the requested quantity does not fit. The remaining quantity has to be processed by a consecutive order. The transmitted quantity represents the actually processed quantity.| | `"TAKEN_OFF_SALE"` | no | The article master data for this `articleNumber` is marked "TAKEN OFF SALE" and therefore the article can not be processed.| | `"OUT_OF_STOCK"` | no | There is not enough stock available, therefore the article can not be processed. | | `"ARTICLE_IS_BLOCKED"` | no | The article master data for this `articleNumber` is marked "ARTICLE IS LOCKED" and therefore the article can not be processed. | | `"NO_SUITABLE_BUNDLE"` | no | KiSoft could not find a suitable pack size for the requested quantity. Thus, the line could not be processed (e.g. requested quantity: 3 - smallest pack size in stock: 5). | | `"SPLIT_CONFIG_ERROR"` | no | Splitting the line was not successful. The split configuration has to be reviewed. | | `"INVALID_BUNDLE_DIMENSIONS"` |no | The requested pack size does not fit into the biggest available load unit. Thus, the line can not be processed.| | `"CANCELLED"` |no | Line was canceled |

GoodsOutLineProcessingResult.description:
States the processing status of an order line
The following status values are possible: |
Value
| Line was / will be processed |Description | |----------|--------------------|---------------------------| | `"UNTOUCHED"` | yes | Line was not processed. | | `"PROCESSED"` | yes |The requested quantity was fulfilled without errors or with all errors remedied during processing. | | `"QUANTITY_UNTRUSTED"`| yes | Technical errors occurred during processing. The actual processed quantity **might not** match the requested quantity. The transmitted quantity represents the best known processed quantity but the number might be incorrect.| | `"QUANTITY_ERROR"` | yes |Processing was not successful. The actual processed quantity **does not** match the requested quantity. The transmitted quantity represents the actually processed quantity. | | `"COUNT_ERROR"` | yes |The quantity was counted and corrected at a check station to a quantity other than the requested quantity. The transmitted quantity is the final processed quantity for the outbound delivery. | | `"UNKNOWN_ARTICLE"` | no | The `articleNumber` transmitted is unknown. The line will not be processed | | `"ZERO_REQUESTED"` | no | The `requestedQuantity` transmitted was zero | | `"MAX_PICK_QUANTITY"` | no | The line exceeded the maximum pick quantity. The transmitted quantity represents the actually processed quantity.| | `"LOAD_UNIT_FULL"`| no | The load unit got full during processing, the requested quantity does not fit. The remaining quantity has to be processed by a consecutive order. The transmitted quantity represents the actually processed quantity.| | `"TAKEN_OFF_SALE"` | no | The article master data for this `articleNumber` is marked "TAKEN OFF SALE" and therefore the article can not be processed.| | `"OUT_OF_STOCK"` | no | There is not enough stock available, therefore the article can not be processed. | | `"ARTICLE_IS_BLOCKED"` | no | The article master data for this `articleNumber` is marked "ARTICLE IS LOCKED" and therefore the article can not be processed. | | `"CANCELLED"` |no | Line was canceled |

GoodsOutLineProcessingError.description: Set if an error occurred during processing.

GoodsOutLineProcessingCode.description: States the processing status of an order line

InboundDeliveryLineProcessingResult.description:
States the processing results of an inboundDeliveryRequestLine. For one line requested, multiple lines may be reported due to different events during processing.
The following status values are possible: |
Value
| Description | |----------|-----------------------------------------------| | `"UNTOUCHED"` | Line was not processed. | | `"QUANTITY_ERROR"` | Errors occurred during processing. The actual received quantity **does not** match the requested quantity. The transmitted `processedQuantity` represents the actually quantity taken into stock. The transmitted `rejectedQuantity` represents the sum of all rejected items during receiving (e.g. because of damaged goods). | | `"PROCESSED"` | Line was processed without errors. The `processedQuantity` represents the sum of received items. | | `"CANCELLED"` | Line was canceled. |

GoodsOutOrderProcessingStatus.description:
Represents the current state of an order.
<details>

![State Diagram](./img/ProcessingStatus-GoodsOutOrder_StateDia.svg)
</details>

OrderProcessingStatus.description:
Represents the current state of an order.
<details>

![State Diagram](./img/ProcessingStatus-Order_StateDia.svg)
</details>

RequestProcessingStatus.description:
Represents the current state of a delivery or request.
<details>

![State Diagram](./img/ProcessingStatus-Request_StateDia.svg)
</details>

DispatchRampNumbers.description: List of dispatch ramps. Every `dispatchRampNumber` *has to be unique* within a warehouse regardless the number of dispatch areas.

DepartureTime.description: Planned departure for the route at dispatch area. In the case a departureTime set for an order and also for the frameworkRoute of this same order, only the departureTime of the order is considered.

DepartureDate.description: Planned departure date for the route at dispatch area.

RampOccupationTime.description: This represents the time , that a ramp is reserved for the orders for this route in the dispatch area.

Priority.description: Gives the priority with which an order or request shall be treated by the system. The higher the number the higher the priority.

PackSize.description: Quantity contained in a pack unit.

CustomerNumber.description: Identifier assigned to a customer by the client.

SupplierNumber.description: Identifier assigned to a supplier by the client.

LoadUnitGrossWeight.description: Weight of the load unit including its content in grams.

LoadUnitIsTouched.description: If `true` the content of the load unit was changed at some time after it was initially stored.

ClientNumber.description:
Many entities in KiSoft are linked to a client by a `clientNumber` like master data, stock or most movement data.

<details>

The warehoused goods are always allocated to a client. Therefore when stock is created, the client is always used as a stock separating attribute.

The process of submitting an order is handled by the client. The client takes its customer purchase orders and transmits this data to the warehouse operating company. The operating company is responsible for the outbound delivery of the goods.

</details>

InboundDeliveryNumber.description: Identifier assigned to an inbound delivery. This information will be used as a general reference when communicating with the higher-level system.

OutboundDeliveryNumber.description: Identifier assigned to an outbound delivery. This information will be used as a general reference when communicating with the higher-level system.

StationName.description: Identifying name of the station. The station name is unique within KiSoft One.

StationStatus.description:
Defines the status of the station.
|
Value
| Meaning | | --- |---| | ACTIVE | A user is logged on to the station. | | INACTIVE | No user is logged on to the station. | | LOCKED | The station is in pause mode. |

ArticleNumber.description: Identifier assigned to the article by the client. For a specified clientNumber the article number has to be unique in the warehouse.

StockType.description:
Project specific defined stock property that is stock separating.
The stock property, stock type, allows separate processes to be mapped for individual stock types (separate picking processes, separate paths through the warehouse, etc.).

LotNumber.description: Identifier of a lot that makes it possible to track the manufacturing process of the lot.

DateMark.description:
Point in time up to which an article retains its characteristics when stored properly.
The term date mark is used in KNAPP texts as a neutral umbrella term for the terms best-by date, use-by date and expiration/expiry date.

SerialNumber.description:
Identifies a single element of an article. For incoming messages (InboundDelivery, StorageOrder) it is only supported for KNAPP-Store and the expectedQuantity must be 1 (otherwise message gets rejected).
The serial number can be numeric or alphanumeric. The serial number is normally marked directly on the element it identifies.

ReservationCode.description: Stock separating property that is used for reserving certain stock for specific processing.

LoadUnitCode.description:
Load unit number in a machine-readable optical code that identifies the load unit.
Generally the load unit code has to be unique for the warehouse.

<details>

The load unit is a logistical unit that can be transported, handled, stored and also picked as a whole.

The forming of standard load units serves to create efficient goods flow processes. Depending on the requirements of the article and the goods flow processes, a load unit can consist of various elements. A typical load unit comprises a load carrier  (e.g. container, carton, pallet, wire box pallet - covered by `loadCarrier`), the cargo located on or in the load carrier (e.g. picked articles).

> Orientation information present in optical codes (e.g. 'L' or 'R' for left and right) that do not represent the load unit number, has to be omitted by all systems.
</details>

LocationZPosition.description:
This value gives information about the storage depth in multiple-deep storage solutions. The higher the value, the further from the aisle the load unit is positioned.
This value is not applicable for flowrack locations.

Slot.description:
Area in a load unit arising from the division of the load unit into smaller areas.

The slot is identified by a number. The slot number identifies the location of the slot on the load unit. The slot number is always a logical entity in the system, physically, it does not have to be applied directly to the slot.

LocationNumber.description: Unique code assigned to the location by the warehouse operating company.

LoadCarrier.description:
Defines the type of the load carrier.
Load carriers are divided by types. Examples of load carriers are: SMALL, MEDIUM, LARGE, FULL, HALF, QUARTER, EURO_PALLET

EventTime.description: Exact time at which the event / status change was recorded.

StockLockReasons.description:
List of stock lock reasons.
|
Value
| Meaning | | --- |---| | DEFAULT | Default stock lock reason | | EXPIRED | Stock was locked, because it is expired | | HOST | Lock set by the higher-level system itself | | LOCATION_LOCKED | Location of the stock is locked | | LOCKED_FOR_VISION_CHECK | Stock was locked for manual Vision Check | | LOST | Lock is added/removed automatically, if a load unit is booked to/from LOST, so the higher-level system knows, that this stock is not allocatable | | QS_REQUIRED | Stock was locked, because it requires a quality assurance check | | SRS_SYSTEM_BROKEN | SRS System Broken | | SUBSYSTEM_LOCKED | Stock was locked by a subsystem | | TIME_TO_EXPIRE | Stock was locked, because the minimum remaining shelf life has expired |

StockBookingReason.description:
The reason why the stock was changed.
For more information, see [Stock Level Relevant Messages](#stock-level-relevant-messages)

Actor.description:
Defines the origin (what triggered the creation) of the reported entity.
|
Value
| Meaning| | --- |---| |`"HOST"` | The reported entity/event was created because of a message from the higher-level system. (e.g. transmitting an `InboundDelivery` message or a `storageOrder` message for pre-received stock )| |`"SYSTEM"`|The reported entity/event was created because of a system internal reason by KiSoft. (e.g. a loadUnit cannot be found anymore and is booked `LOST`) | |`"USER"`|The reported entity/event was created because of an user action by KiSoft. e.g. Creation via GUI |

AdditionalProperties.description: Contains any additional information, that may be displayed or used in a process. The use of additional properties has to be clarified during the planning phase of a project and a development is necessary. If a previously transmitted property has to be deleted, the value of the corresponding key has to be transmitted empty.

key.description: Defines an identifier for the value.

value.description: Any useful information can be put here. (e.g. for display)

ControlFlags.description:
Contains a list of tokens, that represent specific control task that have to be applied to the order.

<details>

The following values are predefined:
| Value | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
|CHECK|The load unit has to be manually checked|
|LID| The load unit has to be lidded |
|STRAP| The load unit has to be strapped|
|BUFFER| The processed load unit has to be stored in a buffer system|
|ROUTE_ASSISTANCE| **Only applicable for storage orders**: requests feedback to the higher-level system about possible entry points to KiSoft the order can use. |

</details>

PlanningStrategy.description: Defines what planning strategy has to be applied for this delivery (project specific defined, useable values must be configured during start-up). Depending on the planning strategy splitting results may differ.

warehouseTasks.description: Warehouse tasks assigned to the station.

userCode.description: User logged on to the station.

LocationClass.description:
The location class classifies locations according to physical criteria.

Example: dimensions, limit load of location.

The location class is available for several location types.

The number of available location classes depends on the warehouse layout.

>**NOTE:** the string has to match the defined location class strings of the given warehouse as defined in KiSoft. Changes and additions can only be done in the KiSoft application.

StorageClass.description:
The storage class classifies storage locations according to logistic criteria.

Example: on which storage locations may cold-storage articles, narcotics or dangerous goods, etc., be stored.

The storage class is only available for storage locations.

>**NOTE:** the string has to match the storage class strings as defined in KiSoft. Changes and additions can only be done in the KiSoft application.

StockQuality.description: The stock quality is not a stock separating attribute and defines if the stock is new or was returned by a customer.

BatchSortCriterion.description: After batch order is picked, items are sorted according to the `batchSortCriterion`. That means that lines with the same value are packed together.

DocumentContent.description: Content of the document to be printed (encoding has to be Base64).

MsgMasterDataUpdateSession.description: This message provides the means to open (set an anchor) and close a session for master data update session transmission.

transmissionTag.description:
`transmissionTag`= `"SET"` sets the start of a session.
If the message is transmitted multiple times for the same client, the session beginning will be reset.
`transmissionTag`= `"CLEANUP"` deletes all data, which has not been transmitted since start of the session with tag `"SET"`.

warehouseTask.description: Defines the warehouse task of the station.

businessCases.description: List of selected [business cases](#business-cases). Empty if not restricted.

workCriteria.description: Work criteria for the station, e.g. DANGEROUS, PRINTER.

Article.description:
Articles are goods of a particular type, that is registered in the stock management system (ERP system, WMS) of a warehouse.
The article object contains information that is spanning across all pack units.

isLotRequired.description: Defines if the lot number is a mandatory information for this article.

isDateMarkRequired.description: Defines if the date mark is a mandatory information for this article.

isSerialNumberRequired.description: Defines if the serial number is a mandatory information for this article.

sizeRange.description: The size range is an index that indicates the available sizes of a clothing item in a table of the customer. Size range 19 is used for socks, which means that sizes 38, 40, 42, 44 and 46 are available

sizePosition.description: The size position indicates the index in the table which contains the exact size (e.g. size position 2 --> size 40)

sizeName.description: Linguistic identifier of a size, e.g. XL, M.

colorNumber.description: Identifier that identifies a color. The term color number is used in KiSoft as a neutral umbrella term used for the various types of numbers that can identify a color (RAL etc.).

salesPrice.description: Sales price for a unit of this article.

HandlingInformation.description: Provides Information necessary for subsystems handling the pack unit.

replenishmentMark.description: The number of items of an article for replenishment for a location. If this stock level falls below the lower stock limit, guided replenishment is automatically initiated.

maxChannelQuantity.description: The maximum stock is the maximum number possible for an article in the article channel of a central belt autopicker. This number is used when calculating the fill level of the article channel.

maxPickQuantity.description:
This data field specifies up to which quantity automated order processing  should still be carried out, from a logistical point of view.
If the requested quantity is higher than the maximum quantity, the entire quantity is picked manually  (i.e. not automated).
Example:
  maxPickQuantity = 10, requestedQuantity = 10, automated picking
  maxPickQuantity = 10, requestedQuantity = 11, non-automated picking

allowBundleRipping.description: If true, ripping bundles for carrying out warehouse processes is allowed.

ejectCode.description:
The ejection number indicates the way in which the pack must be ejected from the autopicker channel. In general, a high ejection number will gently eject the article but reduce ejection capacity.
<details>

|Ejection number	|Speed |
|---|------------------------|
|10	|vmax, continuous          |
|20	|90 % of vmax, continuous  |
|30	|80 % of vmax, continuous  |
|40	|70 % of vmax, continuous  |
|50	|vmax, with intervals      |
|60	|90 % of vmax, with intervals|
|70	|80 % of vmax, with intervals|
|80	|70 % of vmax, with intervals|

KNAPP recommends that the ejection speed is transmitted according to article height and length as a first step. The following values are recommended:
|      Article height     |      Article length     |      Ejection number     |
|-------------------------|-------------------------|--------------------------|
|     10 mm-15 mm         |     <= 100 mm           |     10                   |
|     10 mm-15 mm         |     > 100 mm            |     10                   |
|     15 mm-45 mm         |     <= 100 mm           |     10                   |
|     15 mm-45 mm         |     > 100 mm            |     50                   |
|     45 mm-90 mm         |     <= 100 mm           |     50                   |
|     45 mm-90 mm         |     > 100 mm            |     60                   |
</details>

checkDigit.description: Check digit for pick by voice systems

overstockLocation.description: Defines the position in the warehouse where the overstock can be found.

quantityManaged.description: Declaration, if the article is assigned to a quantity managed location (`"true"`) or a load unit managed location (`"false"`).

rackBay.description: Area in a rack line that includes all rack levels between two rack support.

rackLevel.description: Horizontal area of a rack line system or rack line or rack bay which holds the locations for the load units and/or the articles.

rackChannel.description:
Area in a rack compartment that includes all the load units that stand one behind the other in the rack compartment.

A rack compartment is the area between two rack levels and two rack supports.
<details>

![rack line system top down view](./img/rackline_system_top_view.svg)
</details>

side.description: Defines if a rack line is considered the right side or the left side of a specific aisle.

A rack compartment is the area between two rack levels and two rack supports.
<details>

</details>

preventReplenishment.description: if set to true, replenishment is prevented for this location.

preventPicking.description: if set to true, picking is prevented from this location.

CapacityInformation.description: The list specifies the maximum quantity that can be stored for each specific `loadCarrier`

maximumStoredQuantity.description: Defines the maximum amount of items that can be stored in a specific `loadCarrier`.

PackUnitFull.description: The pack unit defines the article quantity that is held together as a unit using packing material or auxiliary packing aids.

length.description: Length of an article in millimeter.

width.description: Width of an article in millimeter.

height.description: Height of an article in millimeter.

pocketedWidth.description: Defines the width in millimeter for a pocket that may be required if an article is transported in a pocked by an overhead conveyor system.

weight.description: The weight of the article in grams.

opticalCodes.description: A list of values for optical codes representing the pack unit.

articleImageLink.description: URL that points to the image of the pack unit in order to be displayed in various processes. The image file format has to be JPEG. The minimum resolution is 640 pixels in width, the maximum resolution should not exceed 1920 pixels in width.

articleFeatures.description:
Pack units that require or enable special handling and/or storage.
For example: If a pack unit has the `articleFeature` `"COOLING_REQUIRED"` set, this pack unit has to be stored at locations that provide cooling.
If a pack unit has the `articleFeature`  `"ROBOT_TREATABLE"` set, this pack unit may be automatically picked by a robotic system.
How different `articleFeatures` behave in the warehouse has to be configured in KiSoft.

Predefined values:
| Value | Description |
| ------| ----------- |
| AUTOMAT | article is suitable for central belt system |
| COOLING_REQUIRED | article has to be cooled |
| CYLINDRICAL | article is cylindrical  |
| DANGER | dangerous goods that must be stored under certain conditions |
| FRAGILE | article is fragile and may require special treatment |
| HIGHLY_AVAILABLE | article must be highly available in OSR |
| KNAPP_STORE_TREATABLE | article can be stored in KNAPP-Store|
| NARCOTIC | article is a narcotics |
| OSR_TREATABLE | article can be stored in OSR |
| ROBOT_TREATABLE | article can be gripped by Pick-it-Easy Robot |

contentType.description:
Changes the `contentType` of the load unit.
<details>
Allowed changes of contentType - STOCK to EMPTY, EMPTY to STOCK, STOCK to EXTERNAL, EXTERNAL to STOCK, EMPTY to EXTERNAL, EXTERNAL to EMPTY
</details>

loadUnitProperties.description: If this field is transmitted the information contained overwrites all previous load unit properties.

loadUnitStock.description: If this field is transmitted the information contained overwrites all stock information present on the load unit. No stock change messages will be sent to the higher level system.

newPosition.description: States the new position a load unit moved to.

controlReasons.description: list of reasons for control activities.

loadUnitStock.description: Lists all available stock information for this load unit.

orderReference.description: Gives information about the order that is linked to the loadunit.

isLoadUnitEmpty.description: `true` if the whole load unit is empty.

routeAssistance.description: If the higher-level system requested '"ROUTE_ASSISTANCE"' this array lists all possible entry points.

controlReasons.description: list of reasons for control activities.

inboundDeliveryReference.description: Gives information about the inbound delivery that is linked to the received stock.

reason.description: The reason why the stock was changed - "RECEIVE". For more information, see [Stock Level Relevant Messages](#stock-level-relevant-messages)

stockFilterLines.description: All stock that match all criteria presented per filter-line are selected.

orderReference.description: Gives information about the order that is linked to the stock change (if any).

reason.description: The reason why the stock was changed - "ADD_LOCK" or "REMOVE_LOCK".

requestReference.description: Gives information about the request that is linked to the stock change (if any).

reason.description: The reason why the stock was changed - "PICK_ERROR", "QUANTITY_DIFFERENCE", "LOST". For more information, see [Stock Level Relevant Messages](#stock-level-relevant-messages)

extendedReason.description: The additional extended reason entered by the user why the stock was changed - e. g. "TRAY_DAMAGED"

orderReference.description: Gives information about the order that is linked to the stock change (if any).

reason.description: The reason why the stock was changed - "UPDATE_ ATTRIBUTE". For more information, see [Stock Level Relevant Messages](#stock-level-relevant-messages)

allOf.description: Provides information of the state of the stock before the change.

reason.description: The reason why the stock was changed - "MOVE", "DECANT", "MERGE". For more information, see [Stock Level Relevant Messages](#stock-level-relevant-messages)

sourceStock.description: Provides information on the source stock from which a quantity was moved (state of the stock after processing).

StorageArea.description: The storage area for which capacity is reported or stock is located (e.g. "OSR1" for an OSR system).

storageCapacityDetails.description: Gives detailed information about zone capacities.

filledLoadUnitCount.description: Aggregated number of filled storage load units.

emptyLoadUnitCount.description: Aggregated number of empty storage load units.

slotCapacityDetails.description: Gives detailed information about slot capacities.

loadCarrier.description: Gives information about the slot type of the load unit.

filledSlots.description: Number of filled slots for the given 'loadCarrier'

emptySlots.description: Number of empty slots for the given 'loadCarrier'

ContentType.description:
Declares the content and use of the load unit that is to be stored.

<details>

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| EMPTY | Empty load unit without any content. |
| STOCK | Load unit with stock that can be picked from. |
| GOODS_OUT | Load unit with or without already picked stock. The stock (if present) is not available for picking.   |
| EXTERNAL | Load unit with or without stock information. Load unit is maintained by an external system. |

</details>

In the case that no stock information is provided for the storage order, KiSoft is unable to determine an appropriate storage class for the load unit.

inventoryRequestLine.description:
>**NOTE**: at least one of the following fields have to be present in `InventoryRequestLine` in order to be valid:
>  - `articleNumber`
>  - `loadUnitCode`
>  - `locationNumber`

Based on the inventory line, inventory orders are created. `InventoryRequestLine` defines filter criteria selecting stock for inventory.

requestedItems.description: Requests specific load units for relocation.

sequence.description:
Defines a sequence in which the requested load units shall be provided.

Lower numbers will be transported first.

Numbers might be duplicated meaning that the sequence for load units with the same number is not relevant.

processedLoadUnits.description: Lists all processed relocations including the final destinations of the load units.

handoverPosition.description: Position in the warehouse where the load unit is handed over to KiSoft

AreaWeights.description:
Weight checks can be given in one of two ways:
- `AreaWeightExpected` gives expected weights (tolerances are applied by KiSoft)
- `AreaWeightLimits` gives min/max weights (tolerances already included)

expectedWeight.description: The weight in gram, that is expected at a given scale.

minimumWeight.description: The minimum weight in gram, that is expected at a given scale.

maximumWeight.description: The maximum weight in gram, that is expected at a given scale.

taskReference.description: Reference number to identify the VAS task.

workZone.description: Defines the work zone required (if any) for this VAS task.

taskReference.description: Reference number to identify the Task.

SelectionStrategy.description:
This strategy defines the selection strategy of articles for the orderline.

<details>
The following values for the strategy are possible:

| Value | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| STANDARD | First FEFO, than FIFO |
| FEFO | First expired first out |
| FIFO | First in first out |
| LEFO | Last expired first out |
| EMPTY_SLOTS | Find load units, that can be emptied |
</details>

lotNumber.description: Identifier of a lot that makes it possible to track the manufacturing process of the lot. At goods-out orders this attribute is only considered for stock selection in storage systems (OSR, KNAPP-Store), otherwise it is ignored.

dateMark.description: Point in time up to which an article retains its characteristics when stored properly. The term date mark is used in KNAPP texts as a neutral umbrella term for the terms best-by date, use-by date and expiration/expiry date. At goods-out orders this attribute is only considered for stock selection in storage systems (OSR, KNAPP-Store), otherwise it is ignored.

providedQuantity.description: Number of single items of an article that *is already present* in the load unit before it was handed over to KiSoft (e.g. because it was picked ). In the case, KiSoft does not have to pick this line, `providedQuantity` has to equal `requestedQuantity`.
